title: Consumers price index (CPI) | Stats NZ
content:
## Consumers price index (CPI)
The consumers price index (CPI) is a measure of inflation for New Zealand households. It records changes in the price of goods and services. It influences interest rates and is used to calculate changes to benefit payments.
Consumers price index, annual percentage change, March 2022–March 2026 quarters

## Monthly consumers price index (CPI)
Stats NZ will publish a monthly CPI from 2027, following funding announced in Budget 2025. This marks a significant step in providing more timely and relevant inflation data for New Zealanders.
The monthly CPI will be in addition to the quarterly CPI, which we will continue to publish.
See Monthly consumers price index – updates on progress for more information.

## Latest information releases

## Consumers price index: March 2026 quarter
The consumers price index (CPI) measures the rate of price change of goods and services purchased by New Zealand households.
